patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: PM repeatedly creates Loop meeting notes after every meeting following the same Agenda → Notes → Decisions → Action Items → Follow-up pattern. Observed across all 32 cycles from weekly syncs through Camp AIR hackathon debriefs. Highest composite score (92). Confirmed institutional pattern with chain linkage to 2 downstream patterns.

patterns.label: Agent Eval Setup, Analysis & Reporting
patterns.llmRationale: Strongest rising pattern in the dataset. Eval strategy and reporting spans all sources: Weekly Evals Sync meetings, CAPE/Project Crucible leadership email threads, inbound Teams Q&A on metrics, and Loop/Excel eval set templates. Highest value score (96) reflecting breadth of strategic impact. RISING with Project Crucible evals-first mindset driving volume.

patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Largest single-signal volume pattern. Dozens of ADO work-item notifications per week (Tasks, Requirements, Bugs, Features, Epics all tagged Evals). PM is subscriber/mentioned recipient, not direct assignee — making this a pure triage and routing task. Highest automation score (95). SATURATED and stable.

patterns.label: Eval Framework Template & Coverage Scaffolding
patterns.llmRationale: Agent Eval Sets Template (Excel) has been shared and updated repeatedly since Nov 2025. Template maintenance, eval scenario design, and coverage scaffolding recur every cycle. Golden Evals v-team bi-weekly review adds structured review pressure. Composite score 89 — high automation headroom.

patterns.label: Weekly Project Status Report Generation
patterns.llmRationale: Weekly status updates in done/next/blockers format, bi-weekly newsletters, Cape Day LT slides, and EOD debriefs all recur on parallel tracks. Teams explicitly noted multiple versions floating and requested consolidation. RISING due to increasing reporting surface area across CAPE, Crucible, and EvalCon.

patterns.label: Eval Design Scoping & Expert Coaching
patterns.llmRationale: PM is the explicit eval guidance authority for FDEs, customers, and cross-org PMs. 10+ inbound Teams pings per cycle asking: how many eval cases are enough, what metrics to use, when in agent lifecycle to run evals. BSI 89 CRITICAL — PM is sole DRI with zero delegation observed for 28+ cycles. RISING as external partner demand grows.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Meeting Cadence Management
patterns.llmRationale: Four weekly anchors (Evals Sync Tue, Project Crucible v-Team Mon, Manager Team Mon, Solution Library Mon) plus Golden Evals bi-weekly Thu are fully recurring. PM dedicates ~115 min/week to these standing meetings alone. Calendar optimization and pre-meeting prep automation has high value given volume. Highest total hours of any stable pattern.

patterns.label: Hackathon & Learning Event Artifact Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Learning and Scaling Fridays (2:05–5:00 PM weekly) plus CAPE Camp AIR multi-day hackathon account for 108+ cumulative hours. Structured standup/debrief workflows, Copilot cowork sessions, CRM/Dataverse hackathon artifacts, and skill-building content all repeat the same capture-and-distribute cycle. RISING as Camp AIR cadence increases.

patterns.label: Eval Tooling CLI & Implementation Troubleshooting
patterns.llmRationale: Engineers and FDEs repeatedly come to PM for hands-on CLI and tooling issues: CSV import/export quirks, feature flags/preview availability, tool_call_accuracy metric support, and CLI vs in-product tradeoffs. Common enough to codify into an FAQ-style diagnostic skill. Reduces PM interrupt load significantly.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (Duplication Gap)
patterns.llmRationale: Multiple versions of eval intro decks, playbooks, and pitch decks (TFM Agent Evals, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5, eval convo decks) created by different team members independently. Meetings explicitly surfaced this as a consolidation gap. A template-driven skill would eliminate parallel effort and enforce single source of truth.

patterns.label: External Partner & Customer Eval Session Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: 13+ hours/week of external partner meetings across 50+ domains (Accenture, HCL, EY, KPMG, Avanade, Cognizant). Each session follows a similar prep → deliver → recap workflow. Highest time-volume pattern after calendar triage. RISING as CAPE expands partner footprint. High value score (91) — direct customer impact.

patterns.label: Cross-Org Navigation & VOC Routing Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: PM functions as an informal routing layer between field, PM, and PG for VOC and eval-related asks. Common requests: who owns this DRI, which MCS Analytics team handles this signal, which eval team to contact. Automating org-knowledge lookup would free PM interrupt time and reduce latency for field engineers.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning
patterns.llmRationale: PM creates a structured daily prep planning page on a recurring morning cadence rather than starting from scratch each day. Files like [Mar 19] Help me prepare for my day indicate a stable personal ops ritual. Automating calendar summary, priority surfacing, and context loading has strong solo-user ROI.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams
patterns.llmRationale: 8+ Teams messages per cycle share GitHub repos, ZIP files, YAML specs, Loop components, and markdown files. PM uses Teams as a distribution hub for working artifacts. A skill that auto-packages, versions, and posts artifacts to the right channels would reduce manual distribution effort.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture & MCP/Skills Scoping
patterns.llmRationale: NEW pattern first seen cycle 29. Recurring discussions about Declarative Agent architecture, MCP server design, skill-creator and PRD generator scoping, CLI vs UI tradeoffs, and WorkIQ integration constraints. PM is the de-facto architecture consultation hub. High value score (90) — strategic technical direction-setting with strong scaling need.

patterns.label: EvalCon Content Planning & Positioning
patterns.llmRationale: NEW pattern first seen cycle 30. Ongoing multi-thread coordination around EvalCon: what CAPE should present, framing blockers, process learnings, submission deadlines, and alignment with leadership. A content-planning and submission-management skill would reduce fragmented coordination across Teams and email.

patterns.label: Security & Compliance Alert Triage
patterns.llmRationale: 7 compliance/security alerts per week from Office365/Purview and Microsoft Digital Compliance. Subjects: File Blocked, Loop workspace attention, administrator elevation/revocation. High automation score (89) but lower value score (58) as alerts are system-generated triage. Good candidate for first-pass auto-classification rule.

patterns.label: Broadcast & Digest Email Classification
patterns.llmRationale: DECLINING — last seen cycle 28 (4 cycles ago). Viva Engage digests and community announcements were previously a distinct signal cluster. Current signals show digest consumption absorbed into background noise. High automation potential (92) but low value (43) — PM has likely routed these to rules-based inbox filtering already.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Ingests meeting transcript, agenda, or Loop doc and emits structured action items with owner and due-date, decisions log, follow-up email draft, and ADO task stubs. Chain head for 2 downstream patterns. 431 occurrences across all 32 cycles.
triggerExamples: Extract action items from Camp AIR standup debrief
Summarize customer partner session into Loop page with decisions
Generate post-meeting recap from CAPE Architecture Call
Create standardized 1:1 notes template with open questions section
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Replaces ~67 hrs of manual note-taking across 5 roles. Chain-links into status reporting and follow-up drafting. Est. ~$156K/yr avoided cost.

candidateSkills.name: eval-report-synthesizer
candidateSkills.description: Ingests eval results, strategy discussions, golden eval specs, and customer session outcomes. Produces structured eval progress reports, actionability analysis, and cross-team summaries. RISING — 501 occurrences, highest value score (96).
triggerExamples: Synthesize eval progress from CAPE Crucible weekly threads
Generate actionability analysis from golden evals working session
Prepare eval strategy overview from Teams discussions for leadership
Create partner eval session debrief from meeting transcript
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves ~121 hrs across 6 roles. Highest value score in corpus (96). RISING. Est. ~$278K/yr avoided cost.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies ADO work-item notifications by type (Task, Bug, Feature, Epic), workstream (Evals, CAPE, Crucible), and urgency (direct assignee vs subscriber). Routes or dismisses accordingly. SATURATED at 983 cumulative occurrences.
triggerExamples: Triage ADO Evals inbox — action-required vs FYI
Which notifications need PM response vs can be auto-archived?
Classify ADO batch by workstream and DRI
Route Bug ###### to correct engineering owner
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates ~34 hrs of manual triage. 983 occurrences SATURATED — highest ROI-per-implementation in corpus. Est. ~$79K/yr.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Codifies PM eval expertise into an interactive advisory skill. Answers questions about eval case count, metric selection, grounding methodology, rubric design, and agent lifecycle timing. Unblocks BSI 89 CRITICAL bottleneck.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases are enough for a 3-intent agent?
Should I use keyword match or semantic grading for this metric?
When should evals happen in the agent development lifecycle?
Walk me through golden eval methodology for a customer session
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Unblocks BSI 89 CRITICAL single-point-of-failure. 263 occurrences RISING. Scales PM expertise to 50+ external partners. Est. ~$130K/yr.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Generates standardized weekly status from Loop meeting notes, Teams threads, email summaries, and calendar data. Resolves the parallel creation gap where 3+ team members independently author different status artifacts for the same work.
triggerExamples: Generate CAPE eval status update in done/next/blockers format
Draft bi-weekly newsletter from this week's meeting notes and emails
Create EOD debrief template from Camp AIR standup notes
Consolidate multiple team status drafts into a single LT-ready summary
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves ~54 hrs across 4 roles. Resolves 5-format parallel creation gap. 177 occurrences RISING. Est. ~$111K/yr.
